State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration
(916) 322-5660

Executive/Legal
322-5901

Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
322-6441

June 16, 1976

Mr. Robert E. Alderman, Jr.
Attorney at Law
23210 Crenshaw Boulevard
Torrance, California 90505

Dear Mr. Alderman:

The Chairman has asked me to respond to your letter of May 18,
1976, requesting the Commission to issue an informal opinion con-
cerning possible conflicts of interest involving the Torrance School
Board and staff.

Government Code §83114 provides, in relevant part:

"Any person may request the Commission to issue an opinion
with respect to his duties under this title...."

The Commission has enacted a regulation concerning the issuance of
opinions, 2 Cal.Adm. Code §18320, which states:

"Opinion requests may be submitted to the Commission by
any person whose duties under the Act are in question or
by that person's representative."

Since you are not a person "whose duties under the Act are in ques-
tion", the Commission will not render an opinion in response to your
request.

There are, as you are probably aware, several remedial avenues
available to someone who believes a violation of §87100 has occurred.
One such avenue is to file a complaint with our Enforcement Division,
and I have referred your letter to that Division. It is the policy
of the Enforcement Division to refer such matters to the appropriate
District Attorney home, you may wish to discuss this matter with
the District Attorney in your jurisdiction, since he is the primary
civil enforcement authority under the Political Reform Act. See
Government Code §91001(b). Of course, any resident of the jurisdic-
tion also may bring suit, as you have done. It has generally been
the Commission's position not to become involved in such litigation.

The image appears to be a mostly blank page with only a few marks visible at the top - what look like small curved marks or symbols, but no clearly legible text to transcribe.

Mr. Robert E. Alderman, Jr.
June 16, 1976                                                Page Two


     The Commission is, however, in the final stages of adopting
an opinion, No. 76-033, William Owen, which may be of interest to
you in analyzing your case. I will send you a copy of that opin-
ion under separate cover when it is approved.

     Thank you for your interest in the Political Reform Act.

                                                 Sincerely,

                                                 Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                                 Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                                 Staff Attorney
                                                 Conflicts of Interest
                                                   Division



KWG:glb
bcc J.I

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or white, with only a few very faint marks visible at the top. The content is not legible enough to transcribe accurately.

May 18, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Post Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814
Attn: Mr. Daniel Lowenstein, Chairman

Re: Hillside Residents Association vs. Torrance Board of Education
(FPPC Opinion Requested)

Dear Chairman Lowenstein,

This letter is written to you on behalf of Mr. Jim Davis and the
Hillside Residents Association. I presently represent said parties
regarding an alleged violation of the Political Reform Act by several
members of the Torrance School Board and Staff.

The issue involved in this case is crucial in that the violators have
taken steps to close an elementary school which would affect hundreds
of young children and the residential property in the surrounding area.
At least six members of the Torrance School Board and Staff own
real estate within an area that would have been financially effected if
they had decided to close the elementary school in that neighborhood
rather that the elementary school which they eventually selected as
the one to be closed.

I have researched the Political Reform Act thoroughly, and very
definitely feel that participation in the decision as to which school
should be closed by said persons was a violation of section 87000
of the California Government Code et seq. and should therefore be
enjoined and declared void. A complete set of my Points and Authorities
and Supportive Declarations is enclosed herewith for your consideration.

Court action in this matter has been requested by means of a complaint
for injunctive relief filed with the Superior Court of Los Angeles. A
temporary restraining order was denied by the court stating there
was no urgency, in their opinion, to enjoin further activity by the
defendants toward the closing of the Hillside School. A preliminary
injunction was also denied by a different judge; however, no reason
whatsoever was given for that denial.

It is very possible that the judge at the preliminary hearing did
not understand either the facts or the law as he stated prior to
the hearing that he had never heard of the Political Reform Act
before.

[illegible]

[The document appears to be a typed letter on aged/yellowed paper. The text is printed in reverse/mirror image, making it extremely difficult to read accurately. The content appears to be several paragraphs of body text, but due to the reversed printing and image quality, I cannot reliably transcribe the specific words without significant guesswork.]

Due to the reversed/mirrored printing in this document image, I cannot provide an accurate verbatim transcription. The text would need to be either re-scanned in the correct orientation or provided in a clearer format to transcribe exactly as instructed.

Myself, associate counsel in this matter, Mr. William McKim, and
the plaintiffs feel very strongly that there has been a violation of
the Political Reform Act, (Government Code Section 87100 and et seq.),
and that the trial judge erred in denying the preliminary injunction.
We are already initiating the necessary steps to appeal the matter
to the California Appellate Court. However, some action must be
taken immediately to avoid irreversible damage and to make a subsequent
decision by the Appellate Court meaningful. The defendants are
already taking steps to transfer students, teachers, administrators;
and to remove all books, desks and other equipment between June 11
and July 30, 1976. Thereafter, for all intensive purposes the school
would then be closed. It would be almost impossible to reverse the
situation for re-opening in September 1976 unless some affirmative
action is taken to preserve the status quo now.

I feel that an informal opinion or advise from the Fair Political
Practices Commission on this situation would not only help us to
perhaps obtain a temporary injunction while the matter is pending
before the Appellate Court, but would also aid the Appellate Court
in the interpretation of the Political Reform Act which is such new
and recent law.

The issues to which we respectfully ask you to direct your attention
are as follows:

A.    Should the six members of the Torrance School Board and
Education Staff have disqualified themselves from making, participating
in or influencing the decision to close the Hillside Elementary School
since they owned real estate valued in excess of $1,000.00 near
two other schools which were considered as alternatives to the closing
of Hillside?

B.    If said persons should have disqualified themselves but did not,
should the decision in which they participated and influenced be declared
void and all further action precipitating from that decision be enjoined?

If I can be of any further assistance to you on this matter, or provide
any additional facts which may be helpful to you, please call me. If
necessary, I would be happy to fly to Sacramento to meet with your
reviewing committee or commission.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Robert E. Alderman, Jr.

REAired
cc: Mr. Delbert Spurlock, Conflict of Interest Division Chief
encl.

                                            - 2 -

[This image appears to be a typewritten document that is heavily faded and difficult to read clearly. The text is printed in reverse/mirror image, making accurate transcription extremely challenging. The document appears to be multiple paragraphs of body text, with what looks like a signature line near the bottom and possibly some text at the very bottom that might be a date or reference number.]

Due to the severe fading, mirror-image printing, and poor legibility of this document, I cannot provide an accurate verbatim transcription without extensive guessing, which would violate the instruction to transcribe exactly what is seen. A clearer or properly oriented image would be needed for accurate transcription.

ROBERT E. ALDERMAN, JR.
Attorney at Law

TELEPHONE
(213) 539-2510

23210 CRENSHAW BOULEVARD
CARRIAGE REALTY BUILDING
TORRANCE, CALIFORNIA 90505

June 17, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Mr. Goshorn, Conflict of Interest Division

Re: Hillside Residents Association
v. Torrance Board of Education

Dear Mr. Goshorn,

This is a follow-up of my letter dated May 18, 1976 to your commission and
our subsequent phone conversations last week. During those phone conversations, you indicated that your Joint-staff was going to be meeting on
June 11 and would render a decision and/or opinion with respect to my letter
of May 18. You were hopeful that you would be able to reply to me early
this week with that opinion.

Whereas I have not received any further communications from your commission, both myself and our homeowners' association are becoming rather
anxious to know whether or not we properly interpreted the Conflict of Interest
Code with respect to the facts in this situation. As indicated in my letter of
May 18, the urgency of this matter is very high since it involves the closing
of an elementary school and the consequential effects on very small children.

I would very much appreciate it if you could advise me of the status on this
issue as it stands with your commission, within the next few days. Thank
you for your assistance.

Very Truly Yours,

Robert E. Alderman, Jr.

REA/wma

CC: Mr. Jim Smith, Correspondence Secretary

I can see this image appears to be a mostly blank document page with two hole punches visible at the top and what look like fold lines or creases. There is no clearly legible text content visible in this image to transcribe.
